summary.intent: Apply remaining PR review fixes to SKILL.md that were not yet addressed by a parallel 'autofix-pr' session running on the same branch.
summary.outcome: Successfully merged the two sessions' changes by rebasing onto the remote (autofix-pr) version and applying the remaining fixes (follow-up comment detection, CI_FAIL comment wording, payload parse description), then pushed to remote.

repo: The pr-watch skill SKILL.md is actively edited by multiple concurrent Claude Code sessions (autofix-pr and this session), requiring coordination to avoid conflicts.
autofix-pr session used an API_FAIL flag approach for API error aggregation; this session used an API_ERRORS counter approach — structurally incompatible, requiring one to be chosen as base.
HAD_ACTIVITY variable rename was already handled by the remote (autofix-pr) session before this session attempted it.

code.finding: autofix-pr had already applied: exit 1→exit 0, PREV_CONFLICT dedup, HAD_ACT=true on conflict, API_FAIL flag for cycle-level error aggregation, CI_FAIL new-only extraction logic, base-branch retrieval steps.
Remaining fixes applied in this session: follow-up comment detection using comments(first:100)+totalCount, CI_FAIL comment wording update, section 3b CI_FAIL payload parse description.

workflow: When rebase conflicts are structurally complex (different implementations of the same feature), aborting the rebase and resetting to remote then re-applying only the delta is cleaner than resolving conflicts inline.
Before re-applying fixes after a reset, always grep/read the remote version to audit what is already done to avoid redundant or conflicting edits.

friction: Rebase conflict was complex due to two sessions choosing structurally different implementations (API_FAIL flag vs API_ERRORS counter) for the same problem, requiring a strategy change mid-rebase.
Had to manually diff remote vs local changes to determine which fixes remained unapplied after resetting to remote — no automated way to reconcile two parallel editing sessions.
